creator: mwisnicki+freebsd@gmail.com
text:
On Sat, Nov 27, 2010 at 10:13,  <dinoex@freebsd.org> wrote:
> I see no benefit in changeing the default options now.
>

Default option of enabling GNUTLS is misleading and does not work.
Configure script just disables gnutls and resulting binary has no
support for ssl.

There should be no option for GNUTLS if it does not work.

Alternatively, following patches from Fedora unbreak GnuTLS:
http://pkgs.fedoraproject.org/gitweb/?p=cups.git;a=blob;f=cups-serialize-gnutls.patch;hb=HEAD
http://pkgs.fedoraproject.org/gitweb/?p=cups.git;a=blob;f=cups-force-gnutls.patch;hb=HEAD

There is also a patch for OpenSSL in Novell's bugzilla:
https://bugzilla.novell.com/show_bug.cgi?id=617026

> If an option WITHOUT_THREADS is useful for an applicatiion,
> please tell here.
>

Don't know about that, but it seems Fedora used to disable threads
before applying patches above and I guess they had a reason to change
it.

>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=150310
>
